2606.20258.title: Editorial Alignment: A Participatory Approach to Engaging Editorial Expertise in LLM-mediated Knowledge Dissemination
2606.20258.paper_summary: The emergence of LLM-driven information services is reshaping the conditions under which public knowledge institutions operate, threatening to absorb the editorial function these institutions exist to exercise. While LLMs offer powerful new affordances for knowledge dissemination, editorial authority is challenged by pretrained LLMs that arrive already aligned with the values and dissemination strategies of their commercial developers. This paper investigates editor participation in re-aligning LLM interfaces to editorial standards through design workshops, in a case study where we design and implement an LLM-enabled encyclopedia interface with a Nordic public knowledge institution. We introduce editorial alignment as a design practice within Participatory AI, framing AI alignment as a design process and positioning the editorial standard as a design artefact that translates editorial practice and values into alignment objectives for technical implementation. Last, we discuss how editorial alignment can create space for ongoing participation and give editors agency in LLM-mediated knowledge dissemination.
2606.20258.paper_summary_zh: LLM 驅動的信息服務的出現正在重塑公共知識機構運作的條件，威脅到這些機構存在的編輯功能。雖然 LLM 提供了強大的新機會來促進知識的傳播，但預訓練的 LLM 挑戰了編輯權威，因為它們已經與其商業開發者的價值觀和傳播策略對齊。本文通過設計工作坊調查編輯參與重新調整 LLM 介面以符合編輯標準的情況，這是一個案例研究，我們在一個北歐公共知識機構設計並實施了一個 LLM 支持的百科全書介面。我們將編輯對齊引入作為參與式 AI 中的一種設計實踐，將 AI 對齊框架視為一個設計過程，並將編輯標準定位為一種設計工件，將編輯實踐和價值觀轉化為技術實施的對齊目標。最後，我們討論了編輯對齊如何為持續參與創造空間，並賦予編輯在 LLM 媒介知識傳播中的主動權。
2606.20258.author: Simon Aagaard Enni et.al.
2606.20258.authors: Simon Aagaard Enni, Malthe Stavning Erslev, Karl-Emil Kjær Bilstrup, Kristoffer Laigaard Nielbo
